summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #430 where some LLMs (e.g. Qwen3-Coder) send cron tool parameters as stringified JSON or splay nested fields flat, causing validation errors.
summary.outcome: Implemented three rescue paths in cron_tool.rs, wrote 5 new tests, handled two rounds of PR review comments, and resolved all conversations. PR #480 merged cleanly after a final clippy fix.

repo: The moltis cron tool validates parameters strictly — `job` must be a `Value::Object`. LLMs that double-serialize objects break this assumption.
tracing is a direct dependency in moltis-tools with no feature gate; `tracing::debug!` can be used unconditionally.
CI runs clippy with `-D warnings` including `clippy::collapsible_if`, so nested `if let` chains must be collapsed using `&&` let-chain syntax.
PR reviews are done via GitHub GraphQL API to resolve threads; `gh api graphql` with `resolveReviewThread` mutation is the mechanism used in this repo.

code.finding: rescue_stringified_object(&mut Value) helper — must use let-chain (`if let A && let B`) not nested `if let` to satisfy clippy::collapsible_if
normalize_job_value and normalize_patch_value call rescue_stringified_object before their match arms
Flat-param rescue in the 'add' arm assembles a synthetic job object from top-level params, excluding ACTION_KEYS (action, id, limit, patch, job)

workflow: Iterative PR review: two rounds of reviewer comments required separate fix commits; resolving threads required GraphQL mutations via `gh api graphql`.
Clippy failures surfaced only in CI (local tests passed) — running `cargo clippy` locally before pushing would have caught the collapsible_if error earlier.
The pattern of replying to each review comment with a summary and then resolving via GraphQL was the consistent workflow for closing conversations.

friction: Clippy `collapsible_if` error only caught in CI, not during local test run — added an extra commit/push cycle at the end.
Resolving PR review threads required GraphQL mutations; the `gh` CLI has no built-in `resolve-thread` subcommand, requiring raw API calls.
The `&mut Value` refactor required touching multiple call sites and temporarily broke compilation due to ownership/borrow mismatches before being corrected.
open_items: No open items — all review threads resolved, tests passing, clippy clean.
